steps.title: Evaluate the new point relative to the old regression line
steps.content:
At $x = 9$, the original regression line predicts:
$$y = -5.6 + 8.8(9) = -5.6 + 79.2 = 73.6$$
The new data point is $(9, 52)$, which lies 21.6 pounds below this prediction.

Since this point is below the established trend and lies at a large $x$ value (outside the original range of $2 \leq x \leq 6$), it will pull the recalculated regression line downward when included in the new dataset.

steps.title: Determine how the regression slope changes
steps.content:
When a data point falls below the current regression line and is added to the dataset—especially at a larger $x$ value—the new line of best fit must shift to accommodate this lower value. This downward shift reduces the slope of the regression line. Therefore, the new slope satisfies $s < 8.8$.

Examining the choices:
  • A) $5.9$ — less than $8.8$ ✓
  • B) $8.8$ — equal to the original slope ✗
  • C) $14.4$ and D) $17.8$ — greater than $8.8$ ✗
Only choice A represents a value less than $8.8$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
